calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Compliance Status Hearing
Off Calendar Jan-12-2026 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lilas Abuelhawa appeared for successor trustee Harry Miller (via Zoom).
Scott Chan appeared for the Attorney General.

The matter was called for a Compliance Status Hearing.

Mr. Chan addressed concerns raised by the Attorney General regarding compliance with the Court's prior orders, including the scope and documentation of fiduciary training and governance matters. Mr. Chan requested additional information regarding classes completed and raised questions related to trust administration and oversight.

Ms. Abuelhawa responded that Mr. Miller had taken classes relating to nonprofit governance and best practices and described efforts undertaken to bring the trust into compliance, including recovery of funds, bonding, and organizational steps. Ms. Abuelhawa stated that Mr. Miller had substantially complied with the Court's prior directives.

After discussion, the Court stated that it was satisfied with the steps taken by the successor trustee to address the issues that prompted the compliance status hearing. The Court took the compliance status hearing off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Compliance Status Hearing
Off Calendar Nov-24-2025 Continued To Jan-12-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Compliance Status Hearing
Off Calendar Nov-10-2025 Continued To Nov-24-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Appointing Harry Freeman Miller As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-14-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lilas Abuelhawa for and with Harry Miller, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Phillip Chan for the Attorney General.

The court acknowledged reviewing the declarations submitted by Mr. Miller and the Attorney General's Office last week. Mr. Chan expressed concerns regarding Mr. Miller's experience and requested that he receive training in nonprofit management to ensure proper trust administration. Mr. Miller agreed to pursue training and outlined his intention to manage the trust with the assistance of legal and accounting professionals.

Following discussion, the court took the petition under submission with the intent to grant. The court clarified the order will be modified to include language stating that Mr. Miller will be required to complete training in nonprofit management and obtain a bond for the trust within 30 days of receipt from the controller's office.

A compliance status hearing was set for November 10, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Mr. Miller was directed to file a status report by October 27, 2025, outlining the steps taken toward nonprofit management training, progress on expanding the board of trustees, and compliance with registration and bonding requirements.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Order Appointing Harry Freeman Miller As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-10-2025 Continued To May-12-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Matthew Angell for and with Harry Miller, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Phillip Chan, Deputy Attorney General.

Mr. Chan raised concerns regarding the status of the trust and inquired about the timeline of when Mr. Miller became aware of his role as successor trustee and when he learned of the trustee's death. Mr. Miller stated that he learned he was named as successor trustee in 1972 while he was a law student. He believed that Ray Taliaferro was appropriately managing the trust. He stated he did not know the exact date Mr. Taliaferro passed away, but as soon as he became aware of his passing, he stepped in to assert his role. He described his experience managing foundations in academic and nonprofit sectors, having worked in development and fundraising roles at institutions such as Stanford University, Georgetown University, and Texas A&M.

The Court recognized Mr. Miller's qualifications but emphasized the need for proper bonding as required by the trust, restoration of the foundation's legal and tax-exempt status, as well as a clear plan for governance in accordance with California Law. The Court instructed the parties to meet and confer to resolve outstanding concerns. Mr. Chan was directed to consider whether they would withdraw or modify their objections following additional clarifications from Mr. Miller.

The court continued the matter to May 12, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for the parties to meet and confer.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
